summary: Install hook drops and executes an encrypted Windows DLL payload, then self-deletes to hide infection
details: On `npm install`, the package's `install` lifecycle script (lib/chart-loader.js) downloads an encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, base64-decodes the result, validates the PE 'MZ' header, writes it to a randomly-named.dll in the OS tmp directory, and executes it with `rundll32.exe <dll> Run` as a detached, hidden, no-stdio process. After payload execution it spawns `node -e` to recursively `fs.rmSync` its own package directory and rewrite the parent project's package.json to delete the `analysis-chart` dependency entry, masking the infection vector from the developer. The combination of attacker-controlled binary fetch, obfuscation (XOR+base64), Windows PE execution at install time, and anti-forensic self-deletion + manifest tampering is unambiguously a supply-chain dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
